zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes是Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk和Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes的女兒，於1426年出生在蘇格蘭班福爾德斯福德。她和Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie至少有1個女兒。她去世於1522年2月4日在蘇格蘭班福爾享年96歲。

nameMeanings.name: Innes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of McInnis , from Gaelic Mag Aonghuis ‘son of Aonghus’.
Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Innes in Urquhart (Morayshire), named from Gaelic inis ‘island’.

variants.name: Ince
meanings: habitational name from Ince in Makerfield (Lancashire), Ince (Cheshire), or Ince Blundell (Lancashire). The placenames derive from Proto-Welsh ïnïs ‘island’.

possibly a post-medieval West Midlands variant of Hints, a habitational name from either of two places called Hints (Staffordshire, Shropshire). The placenames probably derive from Proto-Welsh hïnt ‘way, path’ + Middle English plural -es.

possibly a variant with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s of Inn, from residence at an inn or inns, from Middle English in (singular) or innes (plural) ‘tavern, lodging(s), tenement(s)’. There seems also to have been some confusion with Scottish Innes .

variants.name: Inch
meanings: English: perhaps, at least in some cases, a variant of   Ince  .

variants.name: McInnis
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Aonghuis, a patronymic from the personal name Aonghus (see Angus ). See also McInnes , compare Irish McGinnis .

variants.name: Inness
meanings: Scottish (Durham and Northumberland): variant of   Innes  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes was born in 1426, in Deskford, Banffshire, Scotland as the daughter of Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk and Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes. She had at least 1 daughter with Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie. She died on 4 February 1522, in Banffshire, Scotland, at the age of 96.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes est née en 1426, à Deskford, Banffshire, Écosse, fille de Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk et Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie. Elle est décédée le 4 février 1522, à Banffshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 96 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes wurde 1426 in Deskford, Banffshire, Scotland, als Tochter von Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk und Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 4. Februar 1522 im Alter von 96 Jahren in Banffshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes è nata il nel 1426 nel Deskford, Banffshire, Scotland come figlia di Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk e Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie. Morì il 4 febbraio 1522, nel Banffshire, Scotland, all'età di 96 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Innes(은)는 1426년 스컷랜드 반프실르 데스k퍼드에서 Sir Walter Innes 10th of Innes, of that Ilk(와)과 Euphemia-nic-Hugh Fraser of Lovat, relic of Mackintosh, latterly of Innes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1522년 2월 4일 스컷랜드 반프실르에서 96세의 나이로 사망했다.

У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Sir John Barclay 7th of Kilbirnie. Она умерла 4 февраля 1522 г. в местности банффшир, Шотландия в возрасте 96 лет.
